name: Gobi Ki Sabzi - Spicy Cauliflower
recipeIngredient: 1 Piece of fresh root ginger, peeled and sliced into thin strips
1 large Cauliflower, trimmed and cut into florets
1/2 Lemon juice (juice from half a lemon)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large saucepan, heat the oil over moderately high heat.
2. Add the mustard seeds to the hot oil and reduce the heat to moderate. Cover the pan and allow the seeds to stop spattering before adding anything else.
3. Remove the lid and add the ginger, onion, turmeric, and green chilli. Stir occasionally for 3 minutes.
4. Add the cauliflower and salt. Stir well to combine.
5. Sprinkle lemon juice over the top of the mixture. Cover the pan, reduce the heat to low, and cook the cauliflower for 20 minutes, or until the florets are tender but not mushy.
6. Turn the contents of the pan into a serving dish and sprinkle with fresh coriander (cilantro) before serving.
This recipe is from Supercook c. 1972/73 and is described as a hot spicy dish of cauliflower flavored with mustard seeds, known as Gobi Ki Sabzi in India. It may be served with chapatis or rice as part of an Indian meal. If desired, Sainsbury's naan bread can be added for extra flavor and texture.
